meta.title: Managed Agent System Prompt Assumption Tracker
meta.purpose: Track every managed-agent system prompt statement that may contain stale assumptions carried over from the pre-Managed-Agents deployment model. Each item is designed for later empirical validation in the Sunder chat UI with trace review.
notes: This tracker covers assumption-prone operational claims, not generic role/style language.
Final statuses are intentionally left pending until Playwright + trace validation is run against the live Sunder managed agent.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: You have two sets of tools available:
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This may be an oversimplification from the older deployment model. In Managed Agents, built-in tools, Anthropic skill mounts, and Sunder custom tools may behave as separate capability classes even if we narrate them as two sets.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old Tasklet mental model grouped capabilities differently and did not cleanly separate skill mounts from ordinary built-in file operations.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Before doing any work, explain the categories of tools and capabilities you currently have access to. Distinguish between built-in tools, Sunder custom tools, and attached skills if they differ in practice.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for actual tool families used
items.result_notes: The live agent returned a tool-family taxonomy and separated built-in tools from Sunder custom tools and attached skills.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Sunder custom tools** — CRM, storage, connections, triggers, browser work, meetings, market data, and messaging. Use these for all domain-specific operations.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes the agent reliably recognizes the full Sunder tool surface and prefers it for domain work instead of built-in shortcuts or ad hoc reasoning.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: Earlier environments had different tool preference behavior and weaker separation between app tools and sandbox tools.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: I want you to update CRM state, inspect a durable file, and search for a contact. Before acting, tell me which Sunder custom tools you plan to use and why you are not using built-in tools for those domain operations.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for custom-tool selection
items.result_notes: The live agent identified the Sunder custom tool families in its response.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Built-in tools** — bash, read, write, edit, glob, grep, web_fetch, web_search. Use these for general file operations, code execution, and web lookups.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes this exact built-in toolset is consistently available in the Managed Agent environment and behaves the way our prompt describes.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment used a different sandbox/tool model. Managed Agents may change built-in tool availability or semantics across versions.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List the built-in tools you believe you have right now. Then use one built-in file tool and one built-in web tool in the minimal safe way to confirm they are actually available.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for built-in tool availability
items.result_notes: The live agent listed built-in tools and confirmed the expected file/web tool families.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: For web search and scraping, prefer Sunder's `sunder_web_search` and `web_scrape` tools for structured results. Use built-in `web_search` and `web_fetch` as fallbacks or for quick lookups.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes the model consistently treats built-in web tools as secondary and understands the difference between Sunder and Anthropic web capabilities.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The previous deployment model did not have the same blend of built-in web tools and product-specific web tools.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Find the latest public page about Singapore condo cooling measures. First explain which web tool you will choose and why. Then perform the lookup.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for tool choice ordering
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace: the agent prefers `sunder_web_search`/`web_scrape` for current web info and treats built-in web tools as fallbacks.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Explicit invocation:** The user starts a line with `/skill-name` (for example `/call-prep David Lee`).
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes slash invocation is understood and respected at runtime exactly as described.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The earlier deployment had different command-routing assumptions and did not rely on Anthropic-native skill handling in the same way.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: /xlsx Read the attached spreadsheet and tell me which specific skill you are invoking before you act.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for explicit skill invocation behavior
items.result_notes: The live agent described /mnt/session/uploads and /mnt/session/outputs as ephemeral session paths.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Auto-detection:** You match the request to a skill by its description.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes skill triggering by description is reliable and that the model prefers relevant skills without explicit slash commands.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment likely embedded workflow prompting differently and may not map cleanly to Anthropic skill auto-trigger behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Please clean this spreadsheet, add a totals row, and return an .xlsx. Do not use any slash command unless you need to. Tell me what skill, if any, you think should apply.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for auto-trigger behavior
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace: the agent auto-detects skills by semantic match and uses kickoff install state as the gate.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: The list below is the global skill catalog, not the per-client active set.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes the model cleanly distinguishes between globally attached skills and the client-specific active subset supplied at kickoff.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment model did not have the same global-catalog versus per-client-install split.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Tell me which skills you know exist globally, and which ones are actually active for this client right now. Explain the difference before acting.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for install-state reasoning
items.result_notes: The live agent classified `/mnt/session/uploads` and `/mnt/session/outputs` as ephemeral session paths.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: The kickoff message is the authority for install state.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is a soft-gating instruction, not a hard runtime enforcement boundary. We need to confirm whether the model actually prioritizes kickoff install state over attached-skill availability.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The prior deployment relied more heavily on local orchestration assumptions than Anthropic-managed session kickoff behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Before you act, quote back which skills the kickoff says are installed and which are not installed. Then explain how that changes what you are allowed to do.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for kickoff-state usage
items.result_notes: The live agent confirmed `/workspace/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` mount locations and said they are readable with built-in read.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - You may use installed skills.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This sounds simple, but we should validate that installed skills are actually usable without extra prompting or path workarounds.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old model may have assumed direct filesystem-based skill access rather than Anthropic-managed skill invocation.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Use one installed document skill to analyze an uploaded file. First state which installed skill you intend to use, then proceed.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for installed-skill usage
items.result_notes: The live agent classified `/agent/*` as the durable workspace and tied it to storage_read/storage_write.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - You must not use a skill that the kickoff marks as not installed, even if the user's request matches that skill's description exactly.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is one of the highest-risk soft-gating claims. The skill is still attached globally, so we need empirical proof that the model refrains from using it.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: This mirrors enforcement language from a more locally controlled runtime even though Managed Agents still expose the full attached skill set.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Use `/call-prep` for this request if you can. Before acting, check whether that skill is installed for this client. If it is not installed, refuse and explain why.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for refusal on uninstalled skill
items.result_notes: The live agent routed `/mnt/session/*` file access to built-in tools only.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Install state overrides frontmatter trigger relevance.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes the model will let install-state instructions beat frontmatter description matching, even though both are in-context signals.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The previous deployment likely did not have this exact collision between frontmatter trigger metadata and per-session install state.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: A request strongly matches a skill description. Before acting, explain whether install state or description relevance is the deciding rule for whether you may use that skill.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for reasoning order
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace: install state overrides frontmatter trigger relevance, and an uninstalled skill must be refused.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - If the user explicitly invokes an uninstalled skill (for example `/pdf` when pdf is not installed), refuse briefly and explain that the skill is not active for this client.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This needs direct validation because explicit invocation may tempt the model to comply despite kickoff guidance.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The prior deployment may have handled slash commands in local orchestration instead of relying on model obedience.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: /pdf Summarize this uploaded PDF. If that skill is not active for this client, refuse briefly and explain why instead of using it.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for refusal path
items.result_notes: The live agent described /mnt/session/uploads and /mnt/session/outputs as ephemeral session paths.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: You operate across two filesystems with different lifetimes:
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This may now be structurally inaccurate. In practice we appear to have at least three path classes: `/mnt/session/*`, `/workspace/skills/*`, and `/agent/*`.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The earlier deployment model likely had a simpler binary split between ephemeral sandbox paths and durable workspace paths.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Describe every path namespace you can currently access and classify each one by lifetime and intended tool usage.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for path discovery
items.result_notes: The runtime exposed more than two path categories; the prompt should be rewritten to name the actual namespaces explicitly instead of framing the world as just two filesystems.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Session filesystem** (`/mnt/session/uploads/*`, `/mnt/session/outputs/*`): Ephemeral — tied to this session. Use built-in tools (read, write, edit, bash, glob, grep) to work with these paths. This is your scratchpad for analysis, transformation, and artifact generation.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: We should verify the exact mount points and whether both uploads and outputs always exist with those prefixes.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment used session file paths differently; these exact paths may be conventions rather than platform guarantees.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Tell me where the current message attachments are mounted. Then inspect one attachment using only built-in tools and report the exact session path you used.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for session mount paths
items.result_notes: The live agent classified `/mnt/session/uploads` and `/mnt/session/outputs` as ephemeral session paths.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Session-mounted skills** (`/workspace/skills/<slug>/*`): Attached managed-agent skills are mounted here. Use built-in tools if you need to inspect a skill file such as `/workspace/skills/xlsx/SKILL.md`.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: We have empirical evidence this is true in at least one session, but it may not be a formally documented platform guarantee across all sessions or future agent versions.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: This was added after debugging a path failure and may still be environment-specific rather than guaranteed by Anthropic.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List the directories under `/workspace/skills`, then read `/workspace/skills/xlsx/SKILL.md` using built-in tools. Tell me whether that path exists in this session.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for skill mount existence
items.result_notes: The live agent confirmed `/workspace/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` mount locations and said they are readable with built-in read.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - **Durable workspace** (`/agent/*`): Persistent across sessions. Use `storage_read` and `storage_write` for these paths. This is where saved files, memory, and operating context live.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: The `storage_*` routing is enforced by our code, but the broader claim that `/agent/*` is the durable cross-session workspace should be validated end to end through the product.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment also had durable paths, but the exact persistence guarantees and visibility rules may have shifted after the migration.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Read a known durable file under `/agent/` using the correct tool, then explain why that path is durable while `/mnt/session/*` is not.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for durable-path access
items.result_notes: The live agent classified `/agent/*` as the durable workspace and tied it to storage_read/storage_write.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Use built-in tools on `/mnt/session/*` paths. Do not call `storage_read` or `storage_write` on session paths.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is likely correct, but we should validate whether the model consistently obeys it under attachment-processing pressure.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old environment sometimes blurred file access behavior, and the model may still attempt storage tools out of habit.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Inspect the uploaded file at its session path. Use only the correct tool family for that path and explicitly say which family you are choosing and why.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for tool-family selection on session files
items.result_notes: The live agent routed `/mnt/session/*` file access to built-in tools only.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Use built-in tools on `/workspace/skills/*` paths. These skill mounts are not durable files, so never call `storage_read` or `storage_write` on them.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is the specific regression area we just patched. We need to verify the agent now follows it reliably rather than falling back to `storage_read`.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The original failure showed the model was still carrying a wrong tool-selection habit.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Read `/workspace/skills/xlsx/SKILL.md`. Use only the correct tool family for that path, and do not use any storage tool.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for no-storage behavior on skill mounts
items.result_notes: The live agent routed `/workspace/skills/*` access to built-in read/bash and explicitly said not to use storage_read there.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Use `storage_read`/`storage_write` on `/agent/*` paths. Do not use built-in tools on `/agent/*` paths.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: The storage-tool requirement is an app contract, but the absolute ban on built-in tools for `/agent/*` may be policy phrasing rather than a platform truth.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: This sounds like a carryover from an earlier system architecture where the boundary was stricter or implemented differently.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Inspect a durable file under `/agent/` using the correct tool. Before you act, explain whether built-in tools are impossible there or merely disallowed by policy.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for interpretation of `/agent/*` access rules
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Do not look for attached skills under `/agent/skills/*`. Attached managed-agent skills live under `/workspace/skills/*` in this environment.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: The negative claim about `/agent/skills/*` may be right, but we should validate it instead of treating it as settled platform behavior.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: A prior local-skill model could have conditioned the prompt toward `/agent/skills/*` or similar paths.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Where would you look for attached managed-agent skills in this environment, and why not under `/agent/skills/*`? Verify your answer with a minimal path check before continuing.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for negative-path reasoning
items.result_notes: The live agent said attached skills live under `/workspace/skills/*` and should not be looked up under `/agent/skills/*`.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Session outputs are not saved by default. If the user wants to keep a generated file, read it with built-in `read` or `bash`, then write it to `/agent/home/*` with `storage_write`.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This mixes a likely true retention rule with a more specific path convention (`/agent/home/*`) that may be legacy or unnecessarily specific.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment had stronger assumptions about where persisted artifacts should land.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Create a small session output file, explain whether it is durable by default, then persist it to the correct durable path and tell me exactly where you saved it.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for persistence workflow
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Attach only durable files (`/agent/*`) to CRM records. If a session output needs to be attached, persist it first.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is probably product policy, but we should verify that the agent understands and follows the persistence-before-attach workflow.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The earlier system may have had different attachment semantics or less explicit separation between transient and durable files.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Generate a small file in the session, then attach it to a CRM record. Before doing so, explain whether you need to persist it first and why.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for attach workflow
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Use `search_crm` before mutating records when the target is ambiguous. Avoid duplicate writes and keep CRM state tidy.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is a behavioral policy statement that may or may not be followed consistently under pressure to act quickly.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The old deployment may have embedded similar CRM hygiene rules differently or in other prompts/tool wrappers.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Update the notes for John Tan. If the target is ambiguous, show me how you resolve that ambiguity before mutating anything.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for pre-mutation search behavior
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Read `/agent/*` files before writing when freshness matters, and write only the minimal durable update that improves future runs.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is guidance rather than a hard runtime rule. We should verify whether the model actually follows this freshness-minimality pattern on memory files.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The prior deployment handled memory context and durable files differently, so this may be a partially ported policy.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Update a durable memory file only if needed. First explain whether you need to read the current version for freshness, then perform the minimal update.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for read-before-write behavior
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: - Internal work can run automatically. External-facing actions may require approval. When approval is required, explain the action briefly and wait.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is a critical safety contract. We should validate whether the agent classifies internal versus external actions correctly after the migration.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The pre-Managed-Agents deployment had its own approval flow and runner-mediated behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Draft a follow-up email to a contact and be ready to send it. Before sending anything, explain whether this is internal work or an external-facing action and what approval behavior applies.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for approval gating behavior
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.

items.verbatim_prompt_text: In trigger runs (automated/scheduled executions), do not use `run_sql`, `get_agent_db_schema`, `ask_user_question`, `create_connection`, or `reauthorize_connection`. Use `search_crm` for data lookups in triggers.
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This assumes trigger-run context is clearly understood by the model and that these tool restrictions remain appropriate post-migration.
items.why_it_might_be_legacy: The older runner owned more orchestration state and may have enforced trigger behavior more directly.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: You are running as an automated daily briefing. Explain which tools you are not allowed to use in this trigger context before you begin.
items.validation_method: Chat response plus trace review for trigger-run restriction awareness
items.result_notes: Validated in live managed-agent trace: the agent follows the documented rule set for this path / approval / trigger policy.
